ticket.title: Add weekly + monthly recurrence support to tasks
ticket.description:
Right now `tf add --recur daily:1` works but `tf add --recur weekly:2` and `--recur monthly:1` either crash or silently drop the recurrence on the round-trip through the API.

We need: (1) shared schema accepts weekly+monthly variants; (2) server creates the next instance correctly on completion; (3) CLI --recur flag round-trips through the API and is shown in `tf list -v`.

The RecurrenceRule lives in packages/shared/schemas.ts. Server logic for instance creation lives in packages/server/src/recurrence.ts.

recordedFinalText:
{"files":[{"path":"proposal.md","content":"# Add weekly + monthly recurrence

## Problem

The RecurrenceRule schema and the server's recurrence engine only support daily. Weekly and monthly are claimed in the README but the round-trip silently fails.

## Approach

1. Extend `packages/shared` RecurrenceRule schema to accept weekly and monthly with `interval >= 1`.
2. Implement instance creation in `packages/server/src/recurrence.ts`: weekly = anchorDay-of-week + interval weeks; monthly = anchor day-of-month + interval months, skipping months without that day.
3. CLI `--recur` parses the kind:interval syntax for all three kinds.
4. `tf list -v` shows the recurrence summary line.

## Test plan

Unit tests for each kind covering DST, leap day, end-of-month wrap."},{"path":"tasks.md","content":"- [ ] Extend RecurrenceRuleSchema (weekly, monthly variants)
- [ ] Implement weekly instance creation in recurrence.ts
- [ ] Implement monthly instance creation with end-of-month wrap
- [ ] CLI --recur parser accepts all three kinds
- [ ] `tf list -v` renders recurrence summary
- [ ] DST + leap-day unit tests"},{"path":"specs/recurrence/spec.md","content":"# Recurrence capability

## Rules

- daily: every N days from the last completion timestamp.
- weekly: anchor on the day-of-week of the source task; spawn N weeks later.
- monthly: anchor on the day-of-month; if the target month has no such day (e.g. Feb 30), skip until a month that does.

## Boundary

Use date-fns UTC helpers exclusively. Never compare local times."}],"openQuestions":[],"assumptions":["Anchor day of recurrence is taken from the source task's createdAt, not its due date","end-of-month wrap policy: skip rather than clamp"],"risks":["Existing daily-only tasks may have implicit nulls in the recurrence column that need a backfill"]}
